name: Yazid ibn Muawiya
title: Tyrant Umayyad Caliph
shortDescription: The usurper Umayyad caliph who ordered the forced allegiance from Imam Hussain (AS) and was the main cause of the Karbala tragedy.
fullStory:
Yazid ibn Muawiya was the second caliph of the Umayyad dynasty who came to power after his father Muawiya's death in 60 AH, violating Imam Hasan's (AS) peace treaty.

He was a corrupt, wine-drinking individual with no regard for religious laws. Immediately after gaining power, Yazid ordered the governor of Medina to extract allegiance from Imam Hussain (AS) and to kill him if he refused.

This order laid the groundwork for Imam Hussain's (AS) uprising and his migration from Medina to Mecca and then Karbala. After Ashura, Yazid initially expressed joy at the killing of the Imam and his companions, and insulted the Imam's severed head by striking his lips and teeth with a cane.

However, with the fiery sermons of Lady Zaynab (AS) and Imam Sajjad (AS) in the palace of Sham, his rule's foundations shook, and to escape public pressure, he shifted the blame to Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad. Yazid died suddenly and suspiciously at the age of 38, just three years after Ashura.
quoteArabic: لَعِبَتْ هاشِمُ بِالمُلْكِ فَلا خَبَرٌ جاءَ وَلا وَحْيٌ نَزَلْ...
quote: (Yazid's blasphemous poetry upon seeing the Imam's head:) Bani Hashem played with power; neither did news come from the sky, nor was revelation sent down.
